ui.footerNote: Prototype focus: asking and answering questions for workplace English.

feedback.loadingSectionSubtitle: Fetching the latest content from the shared API.
feedback.sectionNotFoundSubtitle: The requested section is missing from the content model.
feedback.practiceComplete: Practice complete. This prototype stops the dialogue at five lines to keep the exercise focused.
feedback.noScript: This prototype needs JavaScript enabled to render the training screens.
feedback.clarifyEmpty: Describe your work and generate the first missing-detail prompt.
feedback.askAfterEmpty: Add your work context and generate a short talk for follow-up question practice.
feedback.withoutContextEmpty: Generate a generic prompt to start the drill.
feedback.inlineMediaUnavailable: Inline preview is unavailable for this media link.
feedback.coachChatReady: Start a short text chat, get a realistic reply, and receive one coaching note after every turn.
feedback.coachChatCompleted: This short chat round is complete. Restart the coach to try a new scenario or sharper goal.
feedback.coachChatFallback: The live LLM reply is temporarily unavailable, so the prototype is using the built-in coaching fallback.
feedback.coachChatEmpty: Add a work context and a learning goal, then start the chat.
feedback.clarifyNoExamples: Add audio clarify examples in admin to use this exercise.
feedback.clarifyAudioMissing: Upload an audio file for this example in admin.
feedback.speechRecordingUnavailable: Speech capture is not available in this browser yet. Type your question manually or use the public HTTPS preview.
feedback.speechTranscriptEmpty: No speech was transcribed. Try again or edit the question manually.
feedback.clarifyAnswerRequired: Record or type your clarification question before checking it.
feedback.answeringSessionComplete: You completed the 10-question round. Review the improved answers and restart with the same topic or a new one.
feedback.askAfterSampleQuestion: You mentioned the next step. Could you be a little more specific about that?
feedback.missingContext: Begin with a context phrase such as "You mentioned ..." so the speaker knows which point you mean.
feedback.missingFollow: Add a direct follow-up request such as "Could you talk us through ... ?" or "Could you be a little more specific about ...?"

buttons.startOver: Restart session on the same topic

placeholders.clarifyContext: Example: I am a backend engineer working on billing APIs for enterprise clients.
placeholders.askAfterContext: Example: I work on a product analytics dashboard for retention and onboarding.
placeholders.answeringContext: Example: I am preparing a sprint review about the billing rollout, API stability, and next release risks.
placeholders.answeringReply: Type or dictate your answer here.
placeholders.videoPlaceholder: Video rehearsal placeholder for repeat-after-me speaking practice.
placeholders.coachChatContext: Example: I am a backend engineer preparing for stakeholder meetings about billing APIs.
placeholders.coachChatGoal: Example: I want to sound clearer and more confident when I explain blockers or ask follow-up questions.

admin.backupHint: Download or restore a full application backup including code, content, and uploaded media.

practice.clarifyCoachingTip: Use a short apology, repeat the key noun, and stress the missing detail with a WH word.
practice.withoutContextCoachingTip: No context is needed here. Catch the missing detail, keep the key noun, and ask one short question.
practice.askAfterCoachingTip: Start with a context phrase, then add one clear request for more detail.
practice.askAfterSampleQuestion: You spoke about the next step. Could you be a little more specific?

clarifyFeedback.accepted: Nice repair question. You made the question polite and targeted the missing detail clearly.
clarifyFeedback.wrongTarget: Try again with the right WH word. This prompt is best practised with "{target}".
clarifyFeedback.missingFocus: You are close. Repeat the key topic from the sentence so the listener knows what you are clarifying.
clarifyFeedback.missingPolite: Add a short, polite opener such as "Sorry" and keep the question focused.
clarifyFeedback.mismatch: That does not match the target clarification yet. Compare your question with the correct clarification and try again.

askAfterFeedback.accepted: Strong follow-up. You anchored the question in the talk and then asked for a clear extra detail.
askAfterFeedback.missingContext: Begin with a context phrase such as "You spoke about ..." so the speaker knows which point you mean.
askAfterFeedback.missingFollow: Add a direct follow-up request such as "Could you run us through ... ?" or "Could you be a little more specific?"
askAfterFeedback.missingQuestion: Finish the sentence as a real question so it sounds ready for a meeting.
askAfterFeedback.unclearDetail: Use one real detail from the talk instead of filler text or repeated letters before you ask the follow-up question.
askAfterFeedback.offTrack: Keep the question closer to the selected lead-in and follow-up phrase so it stays clear and natural.

good.promptOne: What outcome is your work on {summary} supposed to improve for the team?
good.promptTwo: If that goes well, what concrete next step would you suggest?
good.wrapUp: That sounded like a strong answer. Keep it short, useful, and finish with the next step.
good.coachingTip: Answer directly, add one useful detail, and end with what happens next.

difficult.promptOne: A stakeholder says the plan around {summary} is slipping. How would you explain that without sounding defensive?
difficult.promptTwo: What is the biggest risk in this {topic} right now, and what are you doing about it?
difficult.wrapUp: Nice difficult-answer pattern: acknowledge the concern, share the facts, and explain the recovery step.
difficult.coachingTip: Stay calm, own the facts, and show the recovery plan.

unnecessary.promptOne: Could you list every tiny detail behind {summary} for the whole team right now?
unnecessary.promptTwo: How would you answer briefly and then steer the discussion back to the main point?
unnecessary.wrapUp: Good redirection. Brief answer first, then bring the group back to the useful part of the meeting.
unnecessary.coachingTip: Do not argue. Answer one small part and then redirect to the real objective.

irrelevant.promptOne: Before we continue, can you tell us something unrelated to the main work on {summary}?
irrelevant.promptTwo: How would you bridge back to the actual agenda without sounding rude?
irrelevant.wrapUp: That works well. A soft bridge plus the next relevant point keeps the meeting moving.
irrelevant.coachingTip: Acknowledge the question politely, then bridge back to the business topic.

noContextDeck.expectedQuestion: Sorry, you need approval from WHO?
Sorry, the extra cost could be HOW MUCH?
Sorry, it only works for WHAT?
Sorry, you deployed it to WHERE first?

clarifyExercises.expectedQuestion: Sorry, the project needs WHAT?

clarifyExercises.fragment: We spoke to ... before the handover.
clarifyExercises.expectedQuestion: Sorry, you spoke to WHO?
Sorry, the rollout starts WHEN?
Sorry, the extra budget could be HOW MUCH?
Sorry, the fix only applies to WHAT?

talkFacts: Our team is preparing the next release for an internal operations platform.
We cut manual work by 22 percent in the latest pilot.
We spoke to four stakeholders in Berlin and Warsaw before the review.
The biggest dependency is a shared approval flow scheduled for next month.
We quoted a provisional rollout budget of 18,000 euros.

clarifyExercises.fragment: The release needs another ... before sign-off.
clarifyExercises.expectedQuestion: Sorry, the release needs another WHAT?

clarifyExercises.fragment: We tested the flow with ... users.
clarifyExercises.expectedQuestion: Sorry, you tested the flow with HOW MANY users?
Sorry, the design review is WHEN?
Sorry, the accessibility issue affects WHAT?
Sorry, you shared the prototype with WHO?

talkFacts: The frontend squad is refreshing the dashboard experience for enterprise users.
Our latest prototype reduced the average task time by 31 percent.
We interviewed three pilot customers after the usability review.
The main dependency is the final API contract for filtering and export.
We aim to finish accessibility fixes before the next sprint review.

clarifyExercises.fragment: The service was rolled out to ... first.
clarifyExercises.expectedQuestion: Sorry, the service was rolled out to WHERE first?

clarifyExercises.fragment: We contacted ... in Berlin about the API change.
clarifyExercises.expectedQuestion: Sorry, you contacted WHO?
Sorry, the deployment window is WHEN?
Sorry, the project needs WHAT?
Sorry, the migration could cost HOW MUCH?

talkFacts: The backend team is separating the billing service from the core API.
We reduced failed retries by 17 percent in the latest staging run.
We coordinated the rollout with two platform engineers in Berlin.
The next production window is planned for the second week of May.
The main risk is aligning authentication changes across legacy clients.

clarifyExercises.expectedQuestion: Sorry, the forecast covers HOW MANY markets?
Sorry, you pulled the data from WHERE?

clarifyExercises.fragment: The dashboard needs ... before the demo.
clarifyExercises.expectedQuestion: Sorry, the dashboard needs WHAT?
Sorry, you reviewed the findings with WHO?
Sorry, the uplift could be HOW MUCH?

talkFacts: The analytics team is refining a retention dashboard for product and support leads.
Our first model suggests a 12 percent increase in repeat usage for active customers.
We compared three regions before presenting the latest findings.
The team still needs a cleaner event taxonomy before the next release.
We expect the stakeholder read-out to happen early next quarter.

clarifyExercises.expectedQuestion: Sorry, the roadmap depends on WHAT?
Sorry, you validated the idea with WHO?
Sorry, the pilot starts WHEN?
Sorry, the launch budget could be HOW MUCH?
Sorry, the experiment runs in WHERE?

talkFacts: The product team is shaping a pilot workflow for onboarding new enterprise users.
We saw the strongest demand from finance and operations leads during discovery.
The pilot is planned to start in June with two design partners.
We quoted a first-phase budget of 25,000 euros for research and rollout support.
The biggest open point is prioritising reporting against workflow automation.

clarifyExercises.expectedQuestion: Sorry, the regression affects WHAT?
Sorry, you logged the issue with WHO?
Sorry, the retest is scheduled for WHEN?
Sorry, the failure only appears in WHERE?

clarifyExercises.fragment: The extra test effort could be ...
clarifyExercises.expectedQuestion: Sorry, the extra test effort could be HOW MUCH?

talkFacts: The QA team is focusing on regression coverage for the next platform release.
We found the highest concentration of failures in account permissions and export flows.
A full retest is planned immediately after the next integration build.
The team expects a shorter release sign-off once flaky tests are removed.
The biggest blocker is incomplete acceptance criteria for two high-impact stories.

learningChat.defaultGoal: Practise short, natural English replies in workplace conversations.
learningChat.systemPrompt: You are a supportive English communication coach for IT professionals. First infer the learner's role, the counterpart's role, the real meeting scenario, and the domain context from the learner goal and context. Do not default to candidate mode: if the learner is interviewing someone, conducting a performance review, leading a meeting, running a negotiation, selling, or presenting, keep that perspective consistent. If the selected scenario is generic but the learner context clearly points to interview, standup, review, or one-to-one, follow the context. Reply like the other real person in the scenario, using role-appropriate pressure, priorities, and profession-specific language. After each learner message, move the dialogue forward naturally based on what the learner just said. Do not repeat the same question once it has already been answered. Return JSON with reply, feedback, and suggestions. reply must be concise, realistic, and grounded in the stated context. feedback must coach clarity, tone, professionalism, and context fit. suggestions must be short candidate replies that match the learner's role.
learningChat.openingReplyTemplate: We are practising {scenarioLabel}. I will reply like a real person in that situation and then coach your English after each turn.
learningChat.openingFeedbackTemplate: Keep each message short, direct, and natural. Match your real role in the conversation, use one practical detail, and give one clear next step where possible.
learningChat.closingReplyTemplate: Nice work. That completes this short {scenarioLabel} practice round.
learningChat.closingFeedbackTemplate: Review the feedback above, then restart the chat with a sharper goal or a different scenario.
learningChat.fallbackReplyTemplate: Thanks. In this {scenarioLabel} conversation, I would reply in the correct role, stay polite, and add one practical detail before the next step.
learningChat.fallbackFeedbackTemplate: Try to sound direct, calm, and specific. Stay in the correct role for the situation, then use one sentence for the main point and one for the detail or next action.
learningChat.providerFallbackNotice: The live LLM reply is temporarily unavailable, so the prototype is using the built-in coaching fallback.
learningChat.transcriptModeTextLabel: Text plus speech-to-text in this MVP

meeting.label: Team meeting
starterSuggestions: What result from this update matters most for the team?
What is the biggest risk we still need to manage before the next release?
Which next step do you want the room to confirm today?
fallbackSuggestions: The main result I want to highlight is...
The biggest risk we still need to manage is...
The next step I want to confirm today is...

interview.label: Interview
starterSuggestions: Could you start with the project that best shows your impact?
What was the hardest trade-off in that work?
If you joined us, what would you improve first?
fallbackSuggestions: The project I would start with is...
The hardest trade-off in that work was...
If I joined, the first thing I would improve is...

oneToOne.label: One-to-one
starterSuggestions: Which result from the last period are you most proud of?
Where do you feel you have grown most since the last review?
What support would help you most in the next period?
fallbackSuggestions: The result I would highlight first is...
The area where I grew most was...
The support I need next is...

standup.label: Standup
starterSuggestions: What did you finish since the last update?
What is your top priority today?
fallbackSuggestions: Since the last update, I finished...

qa.label: Q&A session
starterSuggestions: Can you explain the main risk in plain English?
How would you answer a tough follow-up question?
fallbackSuggestions: A short answer I would give is...

answeringSession.reactionRequiredFeedback: Choose the most appropriate reaction phrase before you submit the answer.
answeringSession.answerRequiredFeedback: Type or dictate your answer before you submit it.
answeringSession.missingCapitalFix: Start the answer with a capital letter.
answeringSession.missingPunctuationFix: End the answer with a full stop or question mark.
answeringSession.addDetailFix: Add one concrete detail or next step.
answeringSession.politeToneFix: Use slightly more polite business language.
summaryTipsFallback: Keep the reaction phrase calm and professional before the main answer.
Answer directly, then add one concrete detail or next step.
Check capital letters, articles, and final punctuation before you send the answer.
answeringSession.answeringQuestionSystemPrompt: You create one realistic workplace English question for a learner preparing for a meeting, interview, review, presentation, or negotiation. Infer from the learner topic whether they are speaking as the interviewer or the interviewee, manager or report, presenter or audience member, seller or buyer, lead or contributor, and keep that perspective consistent. Generate the question from the counterpart's perspective, using domain-specific language that fits the learner's profession and scenario. Never echo the learner topic sentence verbatim inside the question. Return valid JSON with only questionText.
answeringSession.answeringQuestionPromptTemplate: Create one {questionType} workplace question for this scenario: {scenario}. Main focus: {focus}. A specific area to use when helpful: {area}. First infer the learner's role and the counterpart's role from the topic. Do not assume the learner is always the person being interviewed or reviewed. Ask the question from the counterpart's perspective, keep it realistic for spoken business English, use profession-specific detail where possible, and never quote the whole learner topic sentence back verbatim. Extra guidance: {guidance}.
answeringSession.answeringEvaluationSystemPrompt: You assess one workplace English answer. Use the learner topic to infer their actual role and meeting situation before scoring. Do not rewrite the answer from the wrong perspective. The improvedAnswer must only correct the learner's typed or recorded answer body. It must be an answer statement, never a question. Never repeat, replace, or prepend any reaction phrase. Return valid JSON with politenessScore, grammarScore, briefFeedback, improvedAnswer, grammarFixes, toneFixes.

good.label: Good question
good.professionalFocus: Answer directly, add one useful detail, and finish with the next step.

reactionFeedback.accepted: Good reaction. You sounded open and ready to answer a useful question.
reactionFeedback.incorrect: Use a welcoming reaction phrase first so the answer sounds constructive and professional.

reactionOptions.text: Thanks, that is a useful question.

fallbackQuestions: Which result should we highlight first in this {scenario}, and why does it matter?
What progress on {area} do you want the room to remember after this {scenario}?
What decision or next step around {area} should we confirm before this {scenario} ends?
good.generatorGuidance: Ask a useful, focused question from the counterpart perspective that helps the meeting move forward.

difficult.label: Difficult question
difficult.generatorGuidance: Ask about risks, delays, blockers, missed expectations, stakeholder pressure, or trade-offs. Keep the challenge realistic for the learner's actual role.
difficult.professionalFocus: Acknowledge the concern, explain the facts calmly, and end with the recovery step.

reactionFeedback.accepted: Good reaction. You sounded calm and ready to address the concern professionally.
reactionFeedback.incorrect: Start with a calm, steady reaction phrase before you explain the facts.

reactionOptions.text: Sure, let me answer that directly.

fallbackQuestions: What is the biggest risk around {area} right now?
What would you say if someone challenged the current plan for {area}?
What still worries you most about {area}?

unnecessary.label: Unnecessary question
unnecessary.generatorGuidance: Ask for excessive detail that is not useful for the current meeting goal, but make the detour plausible for the topic and the counterpart role.
unnecessary.professionalFocus: Answer one small part briefly, then steer the discussion back to the main objective.

reactionFeedback.accepted: Good reaction. You stayed polite and prepared to redirect the discussion productively.
reactionFeedback.incorrect: Use a short, neutral reaction phrase before you redirect the discussion.

reactionOptions.text: That is not really important right now.

fallbackQuestions: Could you go through every tiny detail behind {area} even if the room only needs the main point?
Would you explain the full background to {area} before we agree the next step?
Can you spend time on a low-value detail from {area} right now?

irrelevant.label: Irrelevant question
irrelevant.generatorGuidance: Ask something off-topic or only loosely connected to the current meeting goal, while still sounding like a real person in that conversation.
irrelevant.professionalFocus: Acknowledge the question politely, then bridge back to the agenda without sounding rude.

reactionFeedback.accepted: Good reaction. You acknowledged the question politely and prepared to bridge back to the agenda.
reactionFeedback.incorrect: Choose a softer bridge phrase so the answer sounds polite and controlled.

reactionOptions.text: We already covered that, so let us move on.

fallbackQuestions: Before we continue, could we switch away from {area} and talk about a side topic?
Can we leave {area} aside for a moment even though it is the main topic of this {scenario}?
Would you mind moving away from {area} to something less relevant right now?

sections.eyebrow: Meeting English practice for IT specialists
sections.summary: This first SOFTskills prototype covers question practice for interviews, one-to-ones, agile rituals, Q&A sessions and team meetings.

Explore a text-based coaching chat with the LLM.
sections.summary: This MVP shows how the learner can practise real conversations, receive coaching after each turn, and later expand into speech workflows.

blocks.title: What this MVP demonstrates
blocks.description: The learner can stay in a short text conversation, see the next realistic reply, and receive targeted communication feedback after each message.

materials.title: Expansion path
materials.body: Text chat is live now. The same session contract already reserves space for future speech-to-text input and text-to-speech playback.

blocks.title: Open the live learning chat
blocks.description: Choose a scenario, describe your work context, set a goal, and practise with the AI coach.

materials.title: How it works
materials.body: Each turn has two layers: a realistic reply from the conversation partner and a short coaching note about your English.

sections.title: Practise answering 10 realistic questions with text or speech.
sections.summary: Start with the topic of your meeting, react with the right phrase, answer the question, and review the improved answer after every turn.

blocks.title: How this session works
blocks.description: Choose the right reaction phrase first, then answer the question and review the coaching after every turn.

materials.title: Session flow
materials.body: You will answer 10 questions across good, difficult, unnecessary, and irrelevant question types. The app checks your politeness and grammar after every answer.

blocks.title: 10-question mixed session
blocks.description: Use text or speech, choose the best reaction phrase, and get an improved professional answer every turn.
